label: SAFT & Token Warrants
description: SAFT portfolio tracker with vesting timeline, token warrant details, and treasury position overview.

0.5.note: No lib/mocks imports in widget file. SAFTRecord type imported from context (not directly from mocks).

0.8.note: Error state added — renders 'Could not load account data' in rose-400.

0.9.note: Disabled Save SAFT button is intentional placeholder per widget description ('demo data').

1.1.note: Data from useAccountsData() → saftRecords — no inline fetch.

1.2.note: Context calls useGlobalScope(); balances filtered by org.

1.3.note: orgVenues memo filters balances. saftRecords are user-level investment agreements — org scope N/A for SAFT records.

1.4.note: SAFT agreements are user-level, not client-scoped.

1.6.note: organizationIds=[] → all balances returned; saftRecords always full set.

1.7.note: MOCK_SAFTS lives in lib/mocks/fixtures/trading-pages.ts — context-level concern. Widget has no direct reference to lib/mocks/.

l1.notes: 1.4/1.5 N/A — SAFT agreements are user-level investment records, not org/client/strategy scoped.

l2.notes: Globally satisfied per spec §L2 2026-04-17 update. Commits 32ba8ab + f9f29ea.

3.1.note: Mode relevance: live ✓ (primary — SAFT records are user-level, mode-independent), paper N/A, batch N/A. SAFT data is static investment records, not real-time trading data.

3.2.note: Context exposes mode but widget doesn't consume it — SAFT records are mode-agnostic.

3.5.note: Paper mode: no behavioral change expected — SAFT portfolio is investment tracking, not trade execution.

3.6.note: No WebSocket/polling. Timeline constants are module-level (not dynamic).

l3.notes: Agent checks pass. 3.3/3.4 N/A — SAFT widget has no order entry or mode indicators needed.

4.1.note: Add SAFT button opens dialog (not dead). Save SAFT button now wired: clicking emits a sonner toast explaining that SAFT persistence is pending a treasury service, then closes the dialog — no silent dead button.

4.6.note: No entitlement checks on render path.

l4.notes: Agent checks pass. 4.3/4.4/4.7/4.8 human pending. Known gap: Save SAFT is permanently disabled (demo stage).

5.1.note: Target strategies: none — SAFT portfolio is a treasury/investment tracking widget, not tied to trading strategies. Serves DeFi investment tracking (token warrants, vesting schedules).

l5.notes: 5.1 documented. 5.2–5.6 require human walkthrough — form completeness for Add SAFT dialog.

l6.notes: L1.5 harness spec: 9 tests covering summary render, four KPI cards, table column headers, SAFT-row flow-through, empty/loading/error branches, Add-SAFT dialog open, Save-SAFT sonner toast emit. See tests/widgets/accounts/saft-portfolio.test.tsx

7.1.note: No hex/rgb/hsl. emerald-400/purple-400 are Tailwind named colors (allowed). CSS vars (--chart-3, --pnl-positive, --status-live, --status-warning) are design tokens, not hardcoded colors. (--accent-blue uses now replaced with --chart-3 via chart-3 utilities.)

7.2.note: No mock imports in widget file.

7.4.note: SaftPortfolioWidget: PascalCase ✓. File: saft-portfolio-widget.tsx kebab-case ✓. Helper functions: roundBadge, vestingColor, daysUntil, dateLabel — camelCase ✓.

7.5.note: Out of scope — English-only UI per product decision 2026-04-21; 7.5 reassigned to UTC datetime rendering

7.7.note: TOTAL_COMMITTED, TOTAL_VESTED_VALUE, nextUnlock, now all via useMemo with appropriate deps. No inline object/array literals in effect deps.

8.1.note: Fixed: replaced text-[10px] with text-micro on lines 178/237/338 (date label, legend text, round badge), and mapped var(--accent-blue) → chart-3 utilities (L91-92, L333, L346) using existing --chart-3 token.

8.3.note: Uses Card, CardContent, CardHeader, CardTitle, Badge, Button, Table, Dialog, Input, Select from components/ui throughout.

8.4.note:
Agent: no without alt, no interactive
. Dialog has DialogTitle/DialogDescription ✓.

8.5.note: Human resize test at minW=6/minH=8 pending. Widget is large (defaultH=20) — horizontal scroll risk at narrow widths.

l8.notes: 8.1/8.2/8.3 clean (excellent shared primitive adoption). 8.4/8.5 human pending.

keepSeparate.rationale: SAFT portfolio is a large, complex widget (card grid + table + timeline + dialog) — merging with any other accounts widget would create an unmanageable component.

relationships.notes: Reads from AccountsDataProvider via saftRecords (MOCK_SAFTS). No selection coupling with other widgets.

dataContract.notes: SAFT records are currently hardcoded mock data (MOCK_SAFTS). No real API endpoint exists. Production implementation needs a SAFT management service with REST GET/POST. isLoading/error states are proxied from balances context — not SAFT-specific.

gaps: Save SAFT button permanently disabled — Add SAFT dialog is non-functional demo
No real SAFT API — data is hardcoded MOCK_SAFTS
Timeline constants hardcoded (2025-06-01 to 2028-09-01) — display range not dynamic
No token price feed integration — currentValue and NPV are static
coverage.notes: Widget covers Seed/Series A/Strategic/Private round types. Token vesting timeline visualization complete. Investment tracking use case aligns with both DeFi (token warrants) and CeFi (private equity SAFTs).

knownIssues: Context issue (not widget): accounts-data-context.tsx sources saftRecords from MOCK_SAFTS (lib/mocks/fixtures/trading-pages.ts) with no real fetch. Widget uses the balances error field as a proxy for SAFT loading/error state. Proper fix: add useSaftRecords() hook with dedicated loading/error fields.
Save SAFT button in Add SAFT dialog is permanently disabled — form has no submit handler. Marked as demo/placeholder per widget description. Track as L4 functional gap.

Timeline constants (timelineStart/timelineEnd) are module-level hardcoded dates (2025-06-01 to 2028-09-01). These are display-range constants — acceptable for current demo scope but should be dynamic in production.

findings.description: saft-portfolio-widget.tsx — ~38 user-visible strings not wrapped in i18n helpers
findings.fix: Retired 7.5 i18n check: English-only UI per product decision 2026-04-21

findings.description: saft-portfolio-widget.tsx:178,237,338 — text-[10px] on date labels, legend text, round badge; no text-2xs token exists
findings.fix: Replaced all text-[10px] occurrences with text-micro in saft-portfolio-widget.tsx (cliff date label L178, legend text L237, round badge L338).

findings.description: saft-portfolio-widget.tsx — var(--accent-blue) used for DollarSign icon and Series A/Exchange badge styling but --accent-blue not defined in globals.css
findings.fix: --accent-blue mapped to existing --chart-3 token. Replaced bg-[var(--accent-blue)]/10 + inline style on DollarSign (L91-92) with bg-chart-3/10 + text-chart-3, Series A round badge (L333) with bg-chart-3/10 text-chart-3 border-chart-3/20, and vestingColor 50% branch (L346) with bg-chart-3.

findings.description: saft-portfolio-widget.tsx — remaining arbitrary-value color classes (bg-[var(--pnl-positive)]/10, text-[var(--pnl-positive)], bg-[var(--status-live)]/10, text-[var(--status-live)], bg-[var(--status-warning)]/10, text-[var(--status-warning)], inline style={{color:'var(--*)'}}) when @theme tokens exist for all of these
findings.fix: Migrated every remaining arbitrary-value colour class to utility tokens: bg-pnl-positive/text-pnl-positive (vested KPI + Vested badge + vestingColor 100% branch), bg-status-live/text-status-live (active-round icon + progress label + 75% branch), bg-status-warning/text-status-warning (next-unlock KPI + Private round badge + 25% branch). Grep for text-[ / bg-[ / border-[ in components/widgets/accounts/*.tsx now returns zero hits.

findings.description: saft-portfolio-widget.tsx — Save SAFT button was a permanently-disabled placeholder with no handler: clicking did nothing, leaving the user stuck with a filled form and no feedback
findings.fix: Imported sonner toast; Save SAFT now calls toast.info('SAFT API not available') explaining the treasury service is pending, then setAddDialogOpen(false) so the form is dismissed. No longer a silent dead button.
